subject: Product Management
title: Democratizing Experimentation
description: A fast breakdown of Democratizing Experimentation for builders.

hook.text: At Booking.com, copywriters, designers, and support agents all run experiments. Not just engineers. Not just PMs. Everyone.

buildup.text: Democratizing experimentation means giving everyone the tools and permission to test ideas, regardless of their role.

discovery.text: The best ideas often come from unexpected places: a support agent who notices a pattern, a designer with an interface hunch.

twist.text: Democratization needs guardrails: clear standards, automated stat checks, and review for tests affecting revenue.

climax.text: Companies that democratize experimentation test 10× more ideas.

punchline.text: Anyone can have a good idea. Give everyone the tools to test theirs.

quiz.question: What guardrails are needed when democratizing experimentation?
options: Clear standards, automated statistical checks, and review for tests affecting revenue
No guardrails are needed — let everyone experiment freely
Only data scientists should be allowed to run experiments
